content.text: This section provides standardized templates and operational tools designed to support consistent implementation of policies, procedures, and practices across all Eduversal Partner Schools. These templates serve as practical resources that enable principals and school leadership teams to maintain compliance with Indonesian regulations, Cambridge International requirements, and CIS/WASC accreditation standards while ensuring efficient daily operations.
All templates in this section are designed according to the following principles:

items: Compliance-Ready: Templates incorporate required elements for Indonesian MoE, Cambridge, and CIS/WASC requirements
EAL-Friendly: Language is clear and accessible; bilingual versions available where appropriate
Contextually Adaptable: Schools may customize templates to reflect local context while maintaining core elements
Evidence-Generating: Templates create documentation suitable for accreditation evidence portfolios
EduNav-Compatible: Templates align with EduNav platform fields and reporting structures
content.title: 19.1.1 Template Design Principles

content.text: Templates are organized by functional area and cross-referenced to relevant handbook sections. Schools should maintain both digital and physical copies of completed templates according to the records retention requirements outlined in Section 14. The Eduversal Template Repository in EduNav serves as the central location for accessing current versions and submitting completed forms.
content.title: 19.1.2 Template Repository Organization

subsections.title: Strategic Planning and School Improvement Templates

content.text: The following templates support the strategic planning and continuous improvement processes described in Section 4. They are designed to align with CIS Domain A (Purpose and Direction), WASC Category A (Organization for Student Learning), and Indonesian accreditation requirements.

Purpose: To document the school's 3-5 year strategic direction, linking vision and mission to measurable outcomes aligned with Schoolwide Learner Outcomes (SLOs). Cross-Reference: Section 4.2 (Strategic Planning Framework), Section 1.3 (Vision, Mission, SLOs) Template Structure: ![][image11] Usage Notes: The Strategic Plan should be reviewed annually, with a comprehensive revision every 3-5 years. Schools should maintain version history and record evidence of stakeholder consultation. The plan forms the foundation for annual School Improvement Plans (T-02).
content.title: 19.3.1 Strategic Plan Template (T-01)

content.text: Purpose: To translate strategic priorities into actionable annual improvement initiatives with clear success criteria and accountability. Cross-Reference: Section 4.3 (Annual School Improvement Planning), Section 17 (Quality Assurance) SIP Action Plan Format: ![][image12] Required Elements:

items: Link each priority to specific SLOs and strategic plan goals
Include baseline data and target metrics for each success criterion
Specify budget allocation per priority area
Document stakeholder input sources (surveys, focus groups, data analysis)
Schedule quarterly reviews using PDSA methodology (see T-04)
content.title: 19.3.2 School Improvement Plan (SIP) Template (T-02)

content.text: Purpose: To document Plan-Do-Study-Act improvement cycles for systematic problem-solving and continuous improvement. Cross-Reference: Section 4.5 (Monitoring and Evaluation), Section 17.3 (Data Conversations Protocol) Form Structure: PLAN Phase:

items: Problem/Opportunity Statement: [Clear description of issue or improvement opportunity]
Root Cause Analysis: [Data and evidence supporting the identified cause]
Improvement Theory: [Hypothesis \- "If we... then we expect..."]
Action Plan: [Specific steps, responsible persons, timeline]
content.title: 19.3.3 PDSA Cycle Documentation Form (T-04)

content.text: DO Phase:

items: Implementation Date(s): [When actions were carried out]

content.text: STUDY Phase:

items: Results vs. Prediction: [Comparison of actual outcomes to expected]
Lessons Learned: [What worked, what didn't, why]

content.text: ACT Phase:

items: Next Steps: [Standardize, scale up, or try new approach]
Communication Plan: [How findings will be shared]

content.text: These templates support the governance structures and processes described in Section 1. They ensure transparent, ethical, and effective oversight consistent with CIS Domain A (Governance & Leadership) and Indonesian Yayasan requirements.

Purpose: To ensure productive and well-structured board meetings with appropriate attention to strategic, compliance, and operational matters. Cross-Reference: Section 1.4 (Board Operations), Section 1.5 (Decision Matrix) Standard Agenda Structure:

items: Call to order and quorum confirmation
Principal's Report (enrollment, academic outcomes, key initiatives)

content.text: Purpose: To identify and manage potential conflicts of interest among board members, senior leadership, and staff with procurement or hiring responsibilities. Cross-Reference: Section 1.6 (Conflict of Interest Policy), Section 13.4 (Procurement Ethics) Declaration Form Fields: ![][image13] Compliance Note: Annual declarations required for all board members and senior leadership. Update required within 30 days of any material change. Forms are maintained by the Foundation Representative with a copy to the General Manager.
content.title: 19.4.2 Conflict of Interest Declaration Form (T-07)

content.text: These templates are critical tools for maintaining child safety and must be used consistently across all Eduversal Partner Schools. They support Section 9 requirements and align with CIS Domain E (Student Wellbeing) and Indonesian child protection regulations.

Purpose: To provide a standardized format for reporting safeguarding concerns to the Designated Safeguarding Lead (DSL), ensuring consistent documentation and appropriate follow-up. Cross-Reference: Section 9.4 (Recognizing and Reporting Concerns), Section 9.6 (Record Keeping) Form Structure: Part A: Reporter Details

items: Reporter Name: [Full name, role, contact details] \- Required
Date & Time of Report: [When form completed]
Date & Time of Concern: [When concern observed/disclosed] \- Be specific
content.title: 19.5.1 Safeguarding Concern/Incident Form (T-08)

content.text: Part B: Child/Student Details

items: Date of Birth/Age: [DOB and current age] \- Required

content.text: Part C: Concern Details

items: Nature of Concern (select all that apply):
Description of Concern: [Factual account of what was observed, heard, or disclosed. Use child's own words in quotation marks where possible. Record facts only, not opinions or interpretations.]
Context: [Where did this occur? Who was present? Any previous concerns?]
Visible Signs/Injuries: [If applicable, describe location, size, color \- do not photograph]
Immediate Actions Taken: [Actions to ensure immediate safety, who was informed]
Child's Wishes: [If disclosed, what does the child want to happen?]

content.text: Part D: DSL Response (DSL Use Only)

items: Risk Assessment Level: [ ] Low [ ] Medium [ ] High [ ] Critical
Actions Taken: [Referrals made, parent contact, external agencies notified, support plans initiated]

content.text: Critical Reminders:

items: Submit to DSL within same day; if imminent danger, contact emergency services first
Record facts, not interpretations; use exact words where disclosed by child
Maintain strict confidentiality; share only on need-to-know basis
Do not promise the child that you will keep information secret
Do not investigate \- that is the role of DSL and authorities
Retain according to records retention policy (see Section 14)

content.text: Purpose: To record concerns that do not meet the threshold for formal safeguarding referral but may form part of a pattern requiring monitoring. Cross-Reference: Section 9.4.3 (Low-Level Concerns), Section 9.7 (Responding to Allegations) Log Fields: ![][image14]Categories of Low-Level Concerns:

items: Boundary issues (e.g., overly familiar language, excessive 1:1 contact)
Professional conduct concerns (e.g., inappropriate comments, social media contact)
Pattern of minor complaints from students/parents
Concerning attitudes or behaviors toward students
content.title: 19.5.2 Low-Level Concern Log (T-09)

content.text: Review Protocol: DSL reviews the log monthly to identify patterns. Annual review with Principal and HR. Entries retained for the duration of employment plus 10 years.

Purpose: To ensure all recruitment activities incorporate safeguarding considerations at every stage, preventing unsuitable individuals from gaining access to children. Cross-Reference: Section 9.3 (Safer Recruitment), Section 12.2 (Recruitment Process) Pre-Advertisement Stage:

items: Job description includes safeguarding responsibilities and commitment statement
Person specification includes suitability to work with children as essential criterion
At least one panel member trained in safer recruitment (Name: \\\\_)
content.title: 19.5.3 Safer Recruitment Checklist (T-11)

content.text: Application Stage:

items: Application form requires full employment history with gaps explained
Application form includes self-declaration of convictions
Applicant provided with safeguarding policy and child protection information

content.text: Shortlisting:

items: Gaps in employment history identified for exploration at interview
Online search conducted (social media, professional profiles)
Any concerns noted for interview exploration

content.text: Interview Stage:

items: Panel includes at least one safer recruitment trained member
Gaps in employment explored and documented
Candidate's motivation to work with children explored
Concerns about online search findings explored

content.text: Pre-Employment Checks:

items: Right to work in Indonesia confirmed
International criminal check (for expatriates, where feasible)
Reference 1 received using safeguarding pro-forma \- Date: \\\_
Reference 2 received using safeguarding pro-forma \- Date: \\\_
References verified by direct contact with referee

content.text: Induction (Before Unsupervised Access to Children):

items: Code of Conduct read and signed

content.text: Sign-Off:

subsections.title: Inclusion, EAL, and Learning Support Templates

content.text: These templates support the Multi-Tiered System of Supports (MTSS) framework and EAL provisions described in Section 8. They are designed to be practical for schools with significant EAL populations (40-70%) and align with CIS Domain C (Support for Student Learning) and Cambridge access arrangements requirements.

Purpose: To document individualized support plans for students with identified Special Educational Needs (SEN), ensuring targeted interventions and progress monitoring. Cross-Reference: Section 8.3 (SEN Identification and Support), Section 8.6 (Access Arrangements) Student Information:

items: Previous IEP (if any): Yes / No Review Date: \\\\\\_
content.title: 19.6.1 Individual Education Plan (IEP) Template (T-12)

content.text: Present Levels of Performance: ![][image15] Annual Goals (SMART format): ![][image16]Interventions and Accommodations: ![][image17] Classroom Accommodations:
Cambridge Access Arrangements (if applicable):
Progress Monitoring Schedule: ![][image18]Team Signatures: ![][image19]

Purpose: To document EAL student language proficiency levels across skills, track progress, and inform differentiation strategies. Cross-Reference: Section 8.4 (EAL Provision), Section 5.4 (EAL Curriculum Integration) Student Information:
Language Proficiency Assessment: ![][image20] CEFR Level Descriptors:

items: A1 (Breakthrough): Can understand and use familiar everyday expressions and very basic phrases
A2 (Waystage): Can understand sentences and frequently used expressions related to areas of most immediate relevance
B1 (Threshold): Can understand main points of clear standard input on familiar matters
B2 (Vantage): Can understand main ideas of complex text on both concrete and abstract topics
C1 (Effective Operational): Can understand a wide range of demanding, longer texts and recognize implicit meaning
C2 (Mastery): Can understand with ease virtually everything heard or read

content.text: Support Level Required: ![][image21] Differentiation Strategies for Teachers:

items: Use visual supports and graphic organizers

content.text: Progress Notes: ![][image22]

subsections.title: Teaching Quality and Professional Practice Templates

content.text: These templates support the appraisal system and professional development processes described in Sections 7 and 12. They align with the Eduversal DTP (Development of Teaching Proficiency) program and Teacher KPI framework.

Purpose: To provide a standardized framework for lesson observations aligned with Cambridge pedagogy principles and the Eduversal appraisal system. Cross-Reference: Section 7.2 (Teaching Standards Framework), Section 12.4 (Appraisal Cycle) Observation Details:

items: Number of Students: \\\\ EAL Students: \\\\ SEN Students: \\\_
content.title: 19.7.1 Lesson Observation Rubric and Form (T-17)

content.text: Observation Domains and Indicators: Domain 1: Planning and Organization ![][image23] Domain 2: Classroom Management ![][image24]Domain 3: Instruction Process ![][image25] Domain 4: Assessment for Learning ![][image26]Rating Scale:

steps: Unsatisfactory: Performance does not meet basic expectations; significant concerns
Basic: Performance meets minimum expectations; development needed
Proficient: Performance consistently meets expectations; good practice evident
Distinguished: Performance exceeds expectations; exemplary practice

content.text: ![][image27]

Purpose: To document individualized professional growth goals linked to appraisal feedback, school improvement priorities, and career aspirations. Cross-Reference: Section 12.5 (Professional Development), Section 7.4 (Coaching and Mentoring) Teacher Information:
Goal 1: [Professional Practice Goal] Goal 2: [Student Learning Goal] Professional Development Activities: ![][image30]End of Year Review:

items: Goal 1 Outcome: [ ] Achieved [ ] Partially Achieved [ ] Not Achieved
Goal 2 Outcome: [ ] Achieved [ ] Partially Achieved [ ] Not Achieved

content.text: These templates support the assessment framework in Section 6 and examination management in Section 10. They ensure quality assurance and compliance with Cambridge.

Purpose: To document internal moderation activities, ensuring consistent standards in assessment across classes and teachers. Cross-Reference: Section 6.3 (Moderation Protocol), Section 17.4 (Internal Quality Reviews) Assessment Information:
Moderation Panel: ![][image31]Sample Selection:

items: Sample Size for Moderation: \\\_ (minimum 10% or 10 scripts, whichever is greater)
Sample Selection Method: [ ] Random [ ] Stratified by grade boundary [ ] Targeted

content.text: Pre-Moderation Standardization:

items: Anchor samples reviewed (exemplars at each grade level)

content.text: Moderation Findings: ![][image32]Summary:
Actions Required: ![][image33]Teaching Implications:
Sign-Off:

Purpose: To document any irregularity or incident during Cambridge or internal examinations for appropriate reporting and follow-up. Cross-Reference: Section 10.5 (Examination Irregularities), Section 3.6 (Malpractice Reporting) Examination Details:
Incident Details:
Type of Incident:
Description of Incident: [Factual account of what happened, including sequence of events, actions taken, and statements made by candidate(s)] Immediate Actions Taken:
Evidence Attached:
Cambridge Reporting (for Cambridge exams only):

items: Reported to Cambridge: [ ] Yes [ ] No [ ] Not Required

content.text: Follow-Up Actions: ![][image34]Sign-Off:

Purpose: To identify, assess, and mitigate risks associated with educational visits and activities, ensuring student safety. Cross-Reference: Section 15.4 (Trip Risk Assessments), Section 9.8 (Health and Safety) Activity Details:
Participants:

items: Students with Additional Needs: \\\_ (attach individual risk assessments)

content.text: Risk Assessment: ![][image35]Emergency Procedures:
Required Documentation:

items: Copy of itinerary left at school

content.text: Approvals:

Purpose: To maintain a comprehensive register of operational, strategic, and compliance risks with mitigation strategies and monitoring. Cross-Reference: Section 15.2 (Risk Management Framework), Section 4.6 (Risk Analysis) Risk Register Format: ![][image36] Risk Categories:

items: Operational (staffing, facilities, IT systems, supply chain)
content.title: 19.9.2 Risk Register Template (T-31)

content.text: Likelihood Scale:

steps: Rare (may occur only in exceptional circumstances)

content.text: Impact Scale:

steps: Major (serious impact on operations or reputation)
Catastrophic (threatens school viability or student safety)

content.text: Risk Score \= Likelihood × Impact
Review Schedule: Risk register reviewed monthly by SLT, quarterly by Board.

Purpose: To structure productive parent-teacher conferences focused on student progress and partnership. Cross-Reference: Section 16.3 (Parent-Teacher Conferences), Section 6.5 (Reporting to Parents) Pre-Conference Preparation:
Conference Agenda (15-20 minutes):

items: What parents can do at home

content.text: Conference Notes:

Purpose: To document formal complaints and track resolution in accordance with the school's complaints policy. Cross-Reference: Section 16.6 (Complaints Procedure), Section 1.7 (Stakeholder Feedback) Complaint Details:

items: Date Received: \\\\\\_ Method: [ ] Written [ ] Email [ ] In Person
content.title: 19.10.2 Complaints Procedure Form (T-34)

content.text: Nature of Complaint:
Complaint Description: [Detailed account of the complaint as stated by complainant] Desired Outcome: [What resolution is the complainant seeking?] Stage of Complaint:
Investigation:
Resolution:

items: Complainant Satisfied: [ ] Yes [ ] No (escalate to next stage)

content.text: Lessons Learned:
Sign-Off:
The following table summarizes how templates in this section support compliance with key regulatory and accreditation requirements:
Templates generate evidence artifacts that support accreditation visits and internal quality assurance. Schools should maintain completed templates according to the following guidelines.

items: Digital Repository: Maintain completed templates in EduNav document management system with appropriate access controls
Physical Files: Retain original signed documents (safeguarding forms, conflict declarations) in secure filing per Section 14
Retention Period: Refer to records retention schedule in Section 14.5; safeguarding records follow extended retention requirements
content.title: 19.12.1 Evidence Storage and Organization

content.text: Prior to CIS/WASC visits, schools should prepare evidence portfolios organized by domain/category, including:

items: Sample completed templates demonstrating consistent implementation
Aggregated data summaries generated from template inputs
Evidence of template review and improvement cycles
Staff training records on template usage
content.title: 19.12.2 Accreditation Visit Preparation

items: Templates are reviewed annually by Eduversal Academic Team
Schools may propose modifications through the General Manager
Version control maintained in EduNav with change log
Updates communicated through Principal Network meetings and EduNav notifications
content.title: 19.12.3 Template Review and Updates

content.text: — End of Section 19 — For additional templates and updated versions, consult the Eduversal Template Repository in EduNav.
